Applied skill: `skills/unit-tests-FIRST.md`

| **Repeatable** | ✓ PASS | No system clock dependencies; no environment variables; in-memory data is constant; results are deterministic |
| **Self-Validating** | ✓ PASS | All assertions use `expect()` matchers (`toHaveBeenCalledWith`, `toHaveBeenCalledTimes`, `toHaveLength`); no console.log substitutes |
| **Timely** | ✓ PASS | Tests cover only `getUserById` (the changed function) and `getAllUsers` (related); Test 6 is a regression test specifically for the API-404 bug |

**Overall**: FIRST compliant ✓

## Regression Test Explanation

**Test 6: [REGRESSION] string "123" resolves to user (API-404 fix)**

This test specifically validates that the bug fix works:

```javascript
test('[REGRESSION] string "123" resolves to user (API-404 fix)', async () => {
  // Simulate what Express sends: req.params.id is always a string
  const req = mockReq({ id: '123' });  // string, not number
  const res = mockRes();
  await getUserById(req, res);
  // Before the fix: would return 404 (string "123" !== number 123)
  // After the fix: correctly resolves to Alice Smith
  expect(res.json).toHaveBeenCalledWith({
    id: 123,
    name: 'Alice Smith',
    email: 'alice@example.com'
  });
});
```

If this test is run against the pre-fix code (without `parseInt`), it will fail — confirming the test would have caught the bug.
